evidence_slots.expected_guard: writes that set or clear lock control must be restricted by privilege/access control; once locked, bits should not clear except by authorized reset/lifecycle action or authenticated JTAG unlock if intended
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: reglk_wrapper computes en = en_acct && acct_ctrl_i and updates reglk_mem on en && we. Reset condition if ~(rst_ni && ~jtag_unlock && ~rst_9) clears all reglk_mem entries to zero, so asserting jtag_unlock clears lock controls. AXI writes can write arbitrary values when selected reglk_ctrl_i bits are not asserted. One case entry for address 2 preserves reglk_mem[3] into reglk_mem[2], suggesting inconsistent lock behavior.
evidence_slots.path: NoC/AXI -> reglk_axilite_bridge -> reglk_wrapper AXI write path -> reglk_mem -> reglk_ctrl_o -> wrapper reglk_ctrl_i slices
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset/default is unlocked zero. jtag_unlock high causes the reset branch and clears all lock bits. Downstream wrapper slices consume reglk_ctrl; some special we_flag connections can alter lock behavior.
evidence_slots.impact: An authorized-at-the-time AXI requester or an entity able to assert jtag_unlock can clear/reprogram locks and re-enable writes to protected peripheral state.

inspection_records.notes: If jtag_unlock is intended as an authenticated lifecycle unlock, the design still clears locks directly on unlock assertion; authentication strength is not evidenced here.

evidence_slots.subject: Software-visible AXI requester
evidence_slots.expected_guard: only authorized privileged software should configure access-control policy, and policy writes should be protected by reglk_ctrl/lock state
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: acct_wrapper resets every acct_mem entry to 32'hffffffff. Writes occur on en && we where en is en_acct && acct_ctrl_i; acct_ctrl_i is supplied from acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][6] in riscv_peripherals. The write cases are blocked only by selected reglk_ctrl bits. acc_ctrl_o ORs acct_mem[0] with {8{we_flag}}, so we_flag can force low policy bits to 1. acc_ctrl_c includes an alias/inheritance rule for j==5 from peripheral 4.
evidence_slots.path: NoC/AXI -> acct_axilite_bridge -> acct_wrapper -> acct_mem/acc_ctrl_o -> acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][peripheral] -> bootrom/wrapper acct_ctrl_i gates
evidence_slots.state_condition: Reset/rst_6 defaults all permissions to 1. Lower privilege policy depends on acc_ctrl_c indexing, but no explicit privilege comparator is visible. Peripheral 5 can inherit permission from peripheral 4. we_flag_0 can force policy bits high.
evidence_slots.impact: Access policy may start open and can potentially be widened/preserved by requesters allowed under the current policy, undermining separation between privilege levels/peripherals.

evidence_slots.subject: Software-visible AXI requester through pkt_wrapper
evidence_slots.expected_guard: fuse access and data exposure should require privilege/access-control and lock mediation; fuse address selection should be constrained to intended ranges

Read mux defaults rdata to fuse_rdata_i whenever en, and case 4 returns fuse_rdata_i if reglk_ctrl_i[6] is not set. pkey_loc high/low reads are only masked by reglk_ctrl_i[4]/[5]. pkt instance is always requested with req_i(1'b1) and indexed by fuse_addr_o.

evidence_slots.impact: An allowed requester can select arbitrary fuse indices and expose fuse or key-related data; stale/default fuse_rdata may be observable for addresses not explicitly handled.

evidence_slots.subject: software-visible reset requester
evidence_slots.object: rst_1..rst_13, peripheral wrapper state, reglk_ctrl, acc_ctrl, fuse/DMA state
evidence_slots.expected_guard: software-triggered peripheral resets should be writable only by authorized privilege/access policy and should not clear security locks or policy in a way that bypasses protection
evidence_slots.observed_behavior: riscv_peripherals declares rst_1..rst_13 and connects local reset inputs to wrappers. acct_wrapper reset/rst_6 sets access-control memory to 32'hffffffff. reglk_wrapper reset/rst_9 or jtag_unlock clears lock memory to zero. pkt_wrapper rst_5 clears fuse_req/fuse_addr. dma_wrapper rst_8 clears DMA config. Search results show a reset wrapper receives acct_ctrl_i = acc_ctrl_c[priv_lvl_i][13] and reglk_ctrl_i slice, but reset decode/effects were not fully provided. Reset defaults for key security controls are permissive/unlocked.
evidence_slots.path: NoC reset channel -> rst_axi_req/rst wrapper -> rst_1..rst_13 -> wrapper local reset inputs -> security-state reset branches
evidence_slots.state_condition: Local resets are active through expressions such as ~(rst_ni && ~rst_N). acc_ctrl reset defaults all ones; reglk reset defaults zero. If software can assert rst_6/rst_9 through the reset path, it can restore permissive policy/unlocked lock state.
evidence_slots.impact: Authorized or incorrectly authorized reset requests can clear locks/policies and thereby bypass protections after reset; post-reset security defaults are not fail-closed.

evidence_slots.impact: An external/test source can override security controls or widen permissions outside the normal MMIO permission and lock mediation path.
